arts.ttl: PSG to 'spare no resources' in pursuit of former Coventry City star Viktor Gyokeres
arts.sum: Paris Saint-Germain are reported to be working on a formal offer to sign former Coventry City star Viktor Gyokeres from Sporting Lisbon. The 26-year-old striker earned his move to Portugal 18 months ago and has been a huge hit with a goal scoring record that has attracted the attention of the top clubs in Europe.

arts.ttl: Frank Lampard claims first managerial scalp as ex-school mate quits following Coventry City defeat
arts.sum: A club statement read: “Millwall Football Club can confirm a mutual decision has been reached with Neil Harris to step down from his role as head coach following The Lions’ trip to Middlesbrough on Saturday. Frank Lampard has claimed his first managerial scalp after Millwall have announced they are to part company with head coach Neil Harris following Coventry City’s 1-0 victory at The Den at the weekend.

arts.ttl: Carlos Corberan explains Brandon Thomas-Asante stance and reveals transfer near miss
arts.sum: Carlos Corberan has admitted that it simply made sense for West Brom to sell Brandon Thomas-Asante to Coventry City in the summer transfer window, after the club weighed up their options and the offer submitted by the Sky Blues. Corberan has also revealed that he came close to snaring Viktor Gyokeres while he was boss of Huddersfield Town, but that City gazumped the Terriers with a superior offer which then parent club Brighton and Hove Albion accepted.

arts.ttl: Carlos Corberan shares Mark Robins chat and assesses Coventry City switch to Frank Lampard
arts.sum: Carlos Corberan has shared the respect he maintains for the job Mark Robins carried out at Coventry City, as he prepares to go head-to-head with his Sky Blues' successor Frank Lampard when they visit West Bromwich Albion on Wednesday evening (8pm kick off). Corberan, who doesn't see a reason why the current Sky Blues squad assembled cannot be fighting for a place in the Championship's top six come spring time, remembers crossing swords with Lampard during his previous spell in the second tier, as manager of Derby County.

arts.ttl: Can boyhood idol Frank Lampard shape the future of Coventry City's Ben Sheaf?
arts.sum: Asked about the summer speculation linking him to several clubs and whether there was any truth in it, Sheaf admitted: “Yeah, there was stuff in it, for sure, but ultimately I am a Coventry player. Do you think Frank Lampard will influence Ben Sheaf's future at Coventry City?
